formulas.name: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)
formulas.variables: EPR is a policy approach used to promote the recycling of waste and the proper disposal of waste generated by a product.
formulas.notes: EPR is a key principle in e-waste management.

formulas.name: Circular Economy
formulas.variables: Circular economy is an economic model that aims to reduce waste and the consumption of resources by promoting the reuse and recycling of products.
formulas.notes: Circular economy is a key concept in e-waste management.

formulas.name: Waste Hierarchy
formulas.variables: The waste hierarchy is a framework that ranks waste management options from most preferred to least preferred.
formulas.notes: The waste hierarchy is a key principle in e-waste management.

formulas.name: Design for Recyclability
formulas.variables: Design for recyclability is an approach that aims to design products that are easy to recycle and minimize waste.
formulas.notes: Design for recyclability is a key principle in e-waste management.

formulas.name: Polluter Pays Principle
formulas.variables: The polluter pays principle is a policy approach that holds the producer responsible for the environmental damage caused by their products.
formulas.notes: The polluter pays principle is a key principle in e-waste management.
